items.title: WA Police launch probe after video shows officer shoving woman to ground during arrest

children.content: An internal police investigation is underway, after video footage emerged online showing a police officer pushing a woman to the ground in Western Australia's far north.

image.altText: A blurry shot of cars lined up on a street.

gallery.altText: a woman with short hair looks sternly at the camera

thumbnail.altText: a woman with short hair looks sternly at the camera

items.title: Alcoa rejects mercury emissions concerns from its WA refinery

children.content: The US mining giant says increased mercury emissions from its Wagerup refinery are a by-product of higher production, amid a Conservation Council appeal against its operating licence.

image.altText: A large concrete office building with glass windows on a cloudy day, with Alcoa written in large letters.

items.title: Convenience store crackdown to curb 'under the counter' illegal nicotine sales

children.content: The City of Fremantle voted unanimously at Wednesday's council meeting to amend local planning laws, in a bid to stop more convenience stores that sell illicit tobacco products from opening.

image.altText: A collection of illegally sold vapes

items.title: Man charged with murder after death of 69yo man in altercation

children.content: A man dies in hospital after an altercation at a home in Padbury, and police have charged a 39-year-old man who was arrested at the scene with murder.

image.altText: Police present at a crime scene.

items.title: House empty more than a year after 69-year-old renter forced out

children.content: After a lifetime of caring for the elderly, Linda Braam is left battling for a place to call home.

image.altText: An older woman in glasses looks sternly at the camera, as she holds the handle of the front door to a house.

items.title: 'I've got my visa back': Child sex offender allowed to stay in Australia

children.content: Edward James Kilbey's permanent visa was cancelled when he was jailed in 2024 for molesting a nine-year-old girl, but a tribunal has reinstated it due to the 83-year-old UK man's "ill health" and "strong ties" to Australia.

gallery.altText: The silhouette of a woman sitting, bending over with head resting on clasped hands

thumbnail.altText: The silhouette of a woman sitting, bending over with head resting on clasped hands

items.title: Two leaders, two very different reactions to Trump's Iran grandstanding

children.content: If you want a basic measure of the difference between Liberal leader Angus Taylor and Nationals leader Matt Canavan, compare their reactions to US President Donald Trump's extraordinary threat this week that "a whole civilisation will die tonight".

image.altText: Angus Taylor and Matt Canavan

items.title: Super members blindsided by change in fund terms and conditions

children.content: A superannuation investment option spruiked as offering "choice and flexibility" has been shut down without proper notification, leaving members less than two months to sell all their assets.

image.altText: Lauren sits on the couch with laptop in the lap, her hair in a high bun

items.title: Unusable road may be the end of remote community as food supplies dwindle

children.content: A remote Aboriginal community in Western Australia has six weeks' worth of food remaining and no truck driver is willing to travel down the notorious road to reach it.

image.altText: A damaged road with red sand on either side.

items.title: Travelling to Europe? New biometric border rules start today

children.content: Australians travelling to Europe will be fingerprinted and face-scanned at the border from today, as authorities activate a sweeping new digital system designed to track who enters and leaves the continent.

image.altText: A man operates an automated EES kiosk at a european border

items.title: 'Not sustainable': Food charities face tough calls as demand skyrockets

children.content: From shrinking their hampers to closing waitlists, food charities in Western Australia are adapting their services to meet a surge in demand as more people grapple with rising costs.

gallery.altText: Boxes full of fruit and vegetables.
Bev Woolhouse poses with a serious expression next to a basket of fruit.
A woman shopping for fresh produce in a supermarket.

thumbnail.altText: Boxes full of fruit and vegetables.
Bev Woolhouse poses with a serious expression next to a basket of fruit.
A woman shopping for fresh produce in a supermarket.

avatar.alt: A blonde woman named Courtney Withers stands wearing a blazer looking serious.

items.title: The NDIS's 'social licence' isn't just at risk — it's close to collapse

children.content: The NDIS's reputation isn't just at risk of coming under pressure, it's well and truly crumbling, write Nas Campanella and Evan Young.

image.altText: Mark Butler standing in a corridor in a suit

avatar.alt: A vision impaired woman with long hair in a blue dress holding a cane

items.title: Albanese says Singapore, Australia 'have each others' backs ahead of talks

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has touched down in Singapore for talks with his counterpart as he tries to shore up fuel supplies. Follow live.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese, Lawrence Wong

items.title: Weekly news quiz: A historical orbit, emotional support animals and a tiny outback town

children.content: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

image.altText: The right side of a spacecraft lit up by the Sun. A waxing crescent Moon is visible behind it. And then, a crescent Earth

items.title: How one of the world's best ballet dancers ended up in Australia

children.content: The Australian Ballet helped put David Hallberg back together after a grave injury. He now returns that favour and leads our national ballet company with a new verve and energy.

image.altText: David Hallberg stands hands in pockets on stage in all black, with a pair of ballet dancers posing on both sides of him

items.title: The petrol testing that sparked a Consumer Protection investigation

children.content: Oliver Peterson reveals the third party fuel testing that sparked an investigation into Burk Fuel.

gallery.altText: A hand holding a fuel nozzle putting petrol in a white car

thumbnail.altText: A hand holding a fuel nozzle putting petrol in a white car

items.title: Sister pushes to raise awareness of male eating disorders after tragic loss

children.content: Joe Cappello was 25 years old when he died after a 12-year struggle, and the statistics show many more men at are risk.

image.altText: A smiling young blonde woman in a brightly-coloured dress cuddles up with a smiling young dark-haired man for in a big house.

items.title: Crows get home against Blues in the wet in Gather Round opener

children.content: The Adelaide Crows send the fans home happy after ending its losing streak by beating Carlton at a packed Adelaide Oval to start Gather Round. Follow live.

image.altText: An Adelaide AFL player tries to kick the ball near the boundary as he is tackled by a Carlton player in torrential rain.

items.title: Top neuroscientist slams AFL move to back new headgear

children.content: The AFL has backed a new helmet that aims to reduce the risk of concussion, but a top neuroscientist is calling it "bad scientific practice".

image.altText: five helmets placed on a sporting field

items.title: AC Milan, Inter and Juventus to play matches in Perth

children.content: Perth will be the centre of Italian preseason football in August, with some of the game's heavyweights heading to the Western Australian capital ahead of the next European season.

image.altText: Opposing players compete for the soccer ball, while another player loiters in support

items.title: Perth man pays it forward by paying for other motorists' fuel

children.content: WA construction company owner Monty Van der Berg wants to ease the cost of living crisis for Australians, by paying for their fuel one tank at a time.

gallery.altText: a man and woman smile into the camera

thumbnail.altText: a man and woman smile into the camera

items.title: Human bones found on Rottnest Island, investigation underway

children.content: Police are investigating the origin of several human bones that were found on Rottnest Island.

image.altText: A police vehicle parked on a building site.

items.title: Animals on wildlife market more likely to carry human-infecting disease, report finds

children.content: Of the 2,079 mammal species traded worldwide, 41 per cent share at least one pathogen with humans, according to new data released today.

image.altText: four birds sit on a ledge inside a market

items.title: 'There in 10 seconds': Dog rescued after being dragged underwater by crocodile

children.content: Pet kelpie Milli had a lucky escape and survived with only minor injuries after being attacked by a freshwater crocodile at Lake Argyle in WA's remote Kimberley.

gallery.altText: A black kelpie sitting on a concrete floor with a bandaged hind leg.

thumbnail.altText: A black kelpie sitting on a concrete floor with a bandaged hind leg.

items.title: The fourth-year med student from Broken Hill who won the Stawell Gift

children.content: Olufemi Komolafe is Broken Hill's first winner of the Stawell Gift since 1949. The 21-year-old sprinter and med student took out the men’s final at the iconic 2026 foot race in Western Victoria over the Easter long weekend.

image.altText: A young male athelete smiles while holding a large golden trophy.

items.title: Day among early Masters leaders, Scott slips despite eagle

children.content: Jason Day kicks off his Masters tilt with a 3-under opening round, while 2013 champion Adam Scott is among a host of players to card eagles on day one at Augusta.

image.altText: Jason Day in round one of the Masters

items.title: Vehicle rollover on WA mine site kills worker, seriously injures another

children.content: A 38-year-old man dies in a crash on an access road to the King of the Hills mine site, near Leonora.

image.altText: The view from a lookout at a gold mine showing the entrance to an underground portal.

items.title: Herb in cold and flu products linked to life-threatening anaphylaxis

children.content: The TGA says people taking popular cold and flu supplements face the risk of life-threatening anaphylaxis from a herbal ingredient.

image.altText: A woman sits at a table with boxes of medication in front of her

items.title: Woman, now pregnant, jailed for nearly 5 years over hit-and-run death

children.content: An intoxicated driver who killed a woman in a hit-and-run in Mandurah, and was allowed to drive two months later, is sentenced to more than four years in prison.

gallery.altText: A smiling young woman with short dark hair and glasses, dressed in black, sits on a bench chair against a wall with artwork.
